commands.repair_reason: A maintainer or contributor should rebase/rerun CI because the only remaining blocker I found is exact-head CI failure without a narrow defect in this PR diff.; Cleared: No concrete new security or supply-chain regression found; the diff adds bounded reads, digest-only cache keys, uncacheable fail-closed states, and no dependency/workflow changes. (sha=8c4f1f088f6509d56de8e0ac68df17d698341fd4)
commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces mtime-based `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` ready-cache inputs with content-hashed auth-profile outcomes, adds post-write `models.json` drift outcomes, hardens file reads/chmod, and adds regression coverage plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows the cache fingerprint still includes `auth-profiles.json` and output `models.json` mtimes, and the contributor supplied live output exercising the fixed cache-hit, oversize, same-size swap, and symlink drift paths.
commands.review_followup: A maintainer or contributor should rebase/rerun CI because the only remaining blocker I found is exact-head CI failure without a narrow defect in this PR diff.

target.title: perf(models-config): content-hash auth-profiles + models.json drift detection

commands.command: please re-review. round 4 follow-up commit `05fda3d839` addressed all remaining findings: (1) replaced `string | null` hash with discriminated `contenthashoutcome` (absent/hashed/uncacheable) — `uncacheable` never matches even itself, eliminating the `null === null` same-size-swap vector (codex p1); (2) auth-profiles `uncacheable` outcome now bypasses the ready cache entirely on every call — no dead entries, no same-size swap rides stored entry (codex p2); (3) stale cap comment at `models-config.ts:55-56` updated to reflect fail-closed semantics (clawsweeper p3). live runtime proof in prior comment shows all 5 scenarios including sticky-miss under oversize auth-profiles and uncacheable/symlinked models.json re-plan.
